human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for investment-operations boundary discipline, source quality, post-trade and custody evidence handling, corporate-action workflow specificity, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to investment-operations execution work such as allocations, affirmations, SSI validation, custody transfers, settlement blockers, corporate-action processing, reconciliation exceptions, and completion evidence capture.
Authority sources cover SEC T+1 guidance, FINRA transfer and books-and-records rules, DTCC trade-processing and corporate-actions workflow references, and SEC custody-control requirements.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete artifacts, rework queues, completion-evidence refusal, terminology handling, overgeneralized industry-name rejection, conflicting-policy handling, and adjacent-lane routing.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/capital-markets-and-asset-management/investment-operations-specialist/ so the lane stays distinct from portfolio, trading, treasury approval, corporate-action decisioning, and legal lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around portfolio-management drift, approval overrides, legal interpretation, and completion without evidence.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated investment-operations specialist that supports evidence-backed post-trade, custody, transfer, corporate-action, and reconciliation workflows.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
